hero.badge: Learning in public with hands-on progress
hero.name: Jaxongir
hero.subtitle: Cybersecurity Enthusiast
hero.desc: My path started with web development and is moving deeper into cybersecurity. I am learning network security, threat analysis, SIEM tools, and incident response through practical projects.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: I started as someone fascinated by programming and how websites are built. I learned independently through the internet up to around junior level, then my interest grew toward cybersecurity. Now I am a student focused on learning and developing in this field.
about.p2: I currently study Energy Engineering through part-time education at Termez University of Engineering and Agrotechnologies.
about.p3: I work as a technical employee at Turk Global Center clinic. Fixing the software side of computers, medical equipment, and other devices is one of my interests. I also have 7+ months of experience in warehouse management, accounting, and preparing warehouse-related documents.
about.p4: My interests include reading books, especially psychology and personal development, even if I do not fully call myself a bookworm. I enjoy listening to music, especially KONSTA, QARZ, and similar hip-hop artists. I am also interested in sports, and I am working on making running a regular habit. Self-study is one of my favorite interests.
about.p5: Beyond that, I am working on developing inner skills such as appreciating what I have, calmness, the subtle art of not caring too much, and other less commonly discussed personal skills.
about.p6: One of my main goals is to grow through the Google Cybersecurity Certificate program and improve my expertise in network security, threat analysis, SIEM tools, and incident response.

steps.title: Networking
steps.desc: Working through TCP/IP, DNS, ports, protocols, and traffic analysis basics with Wireshark.

steps.title: Linux
steps.desc: Strengthening terminal usage, permissions, processes, services, and security log fundamentals.

steps.title: SIEM & Log Analysis
steps.desc: Next step: analyzing alerts, logs, and suspicious activity with Splunk, Wazuh, or ELK.

steps.title: Incident Response
steps.desc: Practicing detection, initial triage, root-cause thinking, and Blue Team response workflows.
